---
title: "FW 536 — Day 2 afternoon lab (v2 · forest & songbird) — Your Name"
author: "Your Name Here"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output:
  html_document:
    self_contained: true
    toc: true
    toc_depth: 3
    toc_float: true
    code_folding: show
    theme: cosmo
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE, message = FALSE, warning = FALSE)
```

# Day 2 afternoon lab — Linear models problem set

**Problem set version:** **v2 — forest & songbird theme**.
(The companion version is [v3 — aquatic & fisheries](problem_set_v3.html); you choose whichever theme fits your interests.)

This template matches [`problem_set_v2.html`](problem_set_v2.html). Read each problem statement there, write your work below, **Knit** to HTML, and upload the `.html` to the matching Canvas assignment.

For setup help see the [R Markdown tutorial](../rmarkdown_tutorial.html).

---

# Setup

```{r}
# Load packages you'll need. Add or remove as appropriate.
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(MASS)        # for glm.nb
library(effects)
library(emmeans)
```

---

# Problem 6 — Stand age × management interaction on bird species richness

> Forty-eight stands; species richness (a count) modeled as Poisson with predictors stand age (years, continuous) and management (factor: unmanaged, thinned). Fit

**My work:**

```{r}
# Your R code here
```

**Answer / biological interpretation:**


---

# Problem 7 — Detection rate of pileated woodpeckers — variable point-count duration

> Across 90 stations, point counts ranged from 5 to 25 minutes (not all the same). You record total detections per station. Two analysts:

**My work:**

```{r}
# Your R code here
```

**Answer / biological interpretation:**


---

# Problem 8 — Northern spotted owl occupancy from canopy cover

> Logistic regression of site-occupancy on percent canopy cover. Intercept = $-2.0$, slope = $+0.05$ per percent canopy.

**My work:**

```{r}
# Your R code here
```

**Answer / biological interpretation:**


---

# Problem 9 — Cone production vs crown class

> A Gamma GLM with log link gives, for the contrast "dominant vs codominant trees", a coefficient $\hat\beta = +0.55$ with 95% CI $(0.32, 0.78)$ on the log scale.

**My work:**

```{r}
# Your R code here
```

**Answer / biological interpretation:**


---

# Problem 10 — Hermit thrush habitat threshold

> Presence/absence of hermit thrush at 300 sites. Model: glm(present ~ basal_area + shrub_cover, family = binomial). Intercept $-3.0$; basal-area slope $+0.08$ per m²/ha; shrub slope $+0.04$ per percent shrub.

**My work:**

```{r}
# Your R code here
```

**Answer / biological interpretation:**


---

# (Optional) Embed a photo of hand-written work

For problems easier to do on paper, photograph and embed:

```{r, echo=FALSE, eval=FALSE, out.width="60%", fig.cap="Hand-derivation for Problem N"}
knitr::include_graphics("images/problemN_derivation.jpg")
```

(Change `eval=FALSE` to `eval=TRUE` after saving an image. See the [R Markdown tutorial](../rmarkdown_tutorial.html) for details.)

---

# Submission checklist

- [ ] Answered every afternoon problem from `problem_set_v2.html`.
- [ ] Knitted to HTML without errors.
- [ ] Photos (if any) embedded with `self_contained: true` in the YAML.
- [ ] Uploaded the `.html` to the matching Canvas assignment.
